footer.tagline: From product idea to official ISO 14044 documentation.

guide.title: Learn LCA requirements and ISO
guide.subtitle: A professional introduction to Life Cycle Assessment (LCA) and the international standards LCA Compass is built on.
guide.disclaimer: Links point to official publishers (ISO, CEN, EU). Full standard texts are typically licensed and purchased via ISO or your national standards body.

whatIsLca.title: What is LCA?
whatIsLca.p1: Life Cycle Assessment (LCA) is a systematic method to quantify a product's environmental impacts from cradle to grave — or within the system boundaries defined by the study. It tracks material and energy flows, converts them to environmental impact indicators (e.g. climate change, resource use), and interprets results against the study's objectives.
whatIsLca.p2: LCA is not a single number but a structured decision process: goal and scope define what is measured and for whom; the life cycle inventory (LCI) documents inputs and outputs; life cycle impact assessment (LCIA) links data to impact categories; interpretation evaluates uncertainty, sensitivity, and limitations. Results must always be reported with method, assumptions, and data quality.
whatIsLca.p3: In Europe, LCA supports product policy, green procurement, EPD programmes, and corporate climate reporting. ISO 14040 and ISO 14044 are the core international reference standards for how studies are planned, executed, and documented.

goalScope.title: Goal and scope
goalScope.desc: Purpose, functional unit, system boundaries, allocation, and data quality requirements.

inventory.title: Life cycle inventory (LCI)
inventory.desc: Data collection for raw materials, production, transport, use, and end-of-life.

impact.title: Life cycle impact assessment (LCIA)
impact.desc: LCIA method selection and calculation of impact categories (e.g. GWP, eutrophication).

interpretation.title: Interpretation
interpretation.desc: Sensitivity, uncertainty, limitations, conclusions, and recommendations.

guide.standardsIntro: Below are the main normative references for product LCA, EPDs, and footprints. Each link goes to the official standard page at the publisher.
guide.supplementaryIntro: Additional publicly available guidance widely used alongside ISO standards.
guide.startLca: Start an LCA in LCA Compass

iso14040.name: Principles and framework
iso14040.description: Defines principles, concepts, and the overarching framework for LCA and LCI studies.
iso14040.role: Foundation for all product LCAs — describes phases and how they relate.

iso14044.name: Requirements and guidelines
iso14044.description: Operational standard with requirements for goal/scope, LCI, LCIA, interpretation, reporting, and optional elements.
iso14044.role: What LCA Compass validates against — checklist and report structure follow ISO 14044.

iso14025.name: Type III environmental declarations (EPD)
iso14025.description: Principles and procedures for Type III declarations based on LCA.
iso14025.role: Basis for EPD programmes and public communication of LCA results.

iso14027.name: Product category rules (PCR)
iso14027.description: Requirements for developing, reviewing, and registering PCR in EPD and footprint programmes.
iso14027.role: Ensures comparability between EPDs within the same product category.

iso14026.name: Communication of footprint information
iso14026.description: Principles for transparent communication of environmental footprints for products.
iso14026.role: Relevant when LCA results are communicated as footprints beyond a full EPD.

iso14067.name: Product carbon footprint (CFP)
iso14067.description: Requirements for quantifying and communicating product greenhouse gas emissions using LCA methodology.
iso14067.role: Used for carbon footprint reporting; builds on ISO 14040/14044 with focus on GWP.

iso14046.name: Water footprint
iso14046.description: Principles and requirements for water footprint of products, processes, and organizations.
iso14046.role: Supplementary impact dimension when water use/stress must be documented.

iso14071.name: Critical review
iso14071.description: Requirements and guidance for critical review of LCA, including reviewer competencies.
iso14071.role: Required for comparative assertions and recommended for EPD and external publication.

iso14072.name: Organizational LCA (O-LCA)
iso14072.description: Additional requirements for applying ISO 14040/14044 at organizational level.
iso14072.role: Relevant for company-wide environmental profiles — not single-product LCA.

en15804.name: Sustainability of construction works — EPD
en15804.description: European standard for EPD of construction products; core PCR for the building sector.
en15804.role: Often required for construction products in the EU; links ISO 14025 with EN 15804+A2.

ilcd.title: ILCD Handbook (EU JRC)
ilcd.description: European Commission's official LCA handbook: data guidance, quality, review, and reporting — widely used in the EU.

pef.title: Product Environmental Footprint (PEF)
pef.description: EU method and rules for product environmental footprint — relevant for market requirements and EU policy.

report.title: LCA Report
report.subtitle: Prepared in accordance with ISO 14040:2006 and ISO 14044:2006

step1.title: Step 1: Goal & Scope
step1.subtitle: Define the purpose, functional unit and system boundaries for your LCA.
step1.purposeHelper: ISO 14044 §4.2.2 requires the study purpose to be clearly described and communicated to stakeholders.
step1.purposePlaceholder: Describe the specific decision this LCA is intended to support...
step1.intendedAudience: What is the primary reason for conducting this LCA?
step1.comparativeNote: If yes → critical review required per ISO 14044 §6.3. Clearly marked in the report.
step1.fuHelper: The functional unit is the backbone of the entire LCA and makes results comparable across products.
step1.fuPlaceholder: Describe what the product PROVIDES to the user — not what it IS...
step1.fuTextPlaceholder: e.g. 'Track one board game for one year with ≥85% data validity...'
step1.rfHelper: Auto-derived from FU — describe the physical quantity of products/components required
step1.rfPlaceholder: e.g. '850 QR labels per year + 1 admin tablet'
step1.cutOffHelper: Include all inputs contributing more than X% of total mass, energy or environmental impact.

allocationMethods.expandDesc: Expand system boundary to avoid allocation
allocationMethods.physicalDesc: Based on mass, energy or other physical criterion
allocationMethods.economicDesc: Based on market value — only as last resort

ambitions.SCREENING: Quick overview analysis, fewer data requirements

step1.limitationsPlaceholder: What is NOT covered by this LCA? Which assumptions may have a major influence?

step2.title: Step 2: Raw materials
step2.subtitle: Register all materials and components included in the product.
step2.nameHelper: Each material must be precisely identified with trade name and supplier specification.
step2.namePlaceholder: Use the chemical or trade name. Refer to the supplier's product specification.
step2.quantityHelp: Enter the quantity per functional unit (FU). Example: 1 product = sum of all materials. Find data from Bill of Materials (BOM), drawings or supplier specifications. Conversion: 1 tonne = 1000 kg, 1 litre water ≈ 1 kg.
step2.sourceDetailHelper: Provide URL, citation, supplier name or database UUID
step2.emissionHelper: kg CO₂-eq per unit — from database or supplier
step2.recycledHelper: Percentage of the material originating from recycled input (0-100%). E.g. PET bottle with 30% post-consumer recycled = 30. Find data in the supplier's Environmental Product Declaration (EPD) or material certificate.

step3.title: Step 3: Production
step3.subtitle: Register energy consumption, processes and emissions in the production phase.
step3.energyHelper: kWh consumed in the process per functional unit. Read from electricity meter or energy bill ÷ units produced. Example: factory uses 50,000 kWh/month producing 10,000 units → 5 kWh/unit. Conversion: 1 MJ = 0.278 kWh.
step3.waterHelper: Water consumption is an ISO 14044-recommended impact category. Contact the manufacturer for a per-product water account.
step3.wastePlaceholder: Describe waste and by-products and their treatment method
step3.emissionsHelper: Direct process emissions (VOC, NOx, SO₂, PFC) contribute directly to air quality and climate impact. Check SDS sheets for chemicals used.
step3.emissionsPlaceholder: e.g. 'VOC: 0.5 g/L ink, NOx: not quantified'

step4.title: Step 4: Distribution
step4.subtitle: Register transport routes from production to end user.
step4.distanceHelper: Road or sea distance in kilometres from shipping point to recipient. Use Google Maps for road freight or searates.com for sea freight. Example: DK to DE ≈ 600 km by road, DK to CN ≈ 20,000 km by sea.
step4.weightHelper: Total weight of goods including packaging per functional unit. Find data from the logistics company's invoice or packing slip. Conversion: 1 pallet ≈ 15-25 kg tare packaging.
step4.loadFactorHelper: Average load factor for the transport (0-100%). Full load = 100%, half loaded = 50%. EU average for truck ≈ 58%. Lower load factor = higher emission per kg-km.

step5.title: Step 5: Use phase
step5.subtitle: Register energy consumption, lifetime and behavioural assumptions for the use phase.
step5.lifetimeHelper: Lifetime is critical for correctly normalising impact per functional unit. Use the manufacturer's warranty period + typical use from industry data.
step5.energyHelper: Average electricity consumption per year during normal use. Measure with an energy monitor (Kill A Watt) or use manufacturer specification (rated watts × use hours ÷ 1000 = kWh). Example: 10W × 8h/day × 365 days ÷ 1000 = 29.2 kWh/year.
step5.consumablesPlaceholder: e.g. 'Label replacement every 1-2 years', 'AAA batteries x4 per year'
step5.behaviorHelper: User behaviour can have a significant impact on use-phase impact. Describe typical usage patterns based on manufacturer data.
step5.behaviorPlaceholder: e.g. 'User assumed to own a smartphone (>92% of Danes over 16). 8h daily use.'
step5.reboundHelper: Describes unintended increased consumption resulting from improved efficiency
step5.reboundPlaceholder: e.g. 'No rebound effects identified' or 'Energy savings may lead to increased use'

step6.title: Step 6: End-of-Life
step6.subtitle: Define how the product is managed at end of life. Percentages must sum to 100%.
step6.disassemblyNote: Easier disassembly significantly improves recycling rates
step6.disassemblyPlaceholder: e.g. 'Snap-fit connections without glue, standard screws, material labelling'
step6.recoveryRateHelper: Percentage actually recovered (0-100%). Find data in Statistics Denmark, Circular Economy report for your sector, or use EU Waste Framework Directive averages. Plastic in DK: ~30%, Paper/cardboard: ~70%, Metal: ~80-95%.
step6.assumptionsHelper: EOL assumptions must be based on actual systems. Use Danish/EU waste statistics for your product category.
step6.assumptionsPlaceholder: e.g. 'Based on Danish waste statistics 2024' or 'Conservative: product assumed to follow user to bin'
step6.takebackLabel: Is there a producer responsibility/take-back system?

step7.title: Step 7: LCIA configuration
step7.subtitle: Select LCIA method and impact categories.

methodDescs.ReCiPe2016: Most widely used international method. Covers 18 midpoint + 3 endpoint categories.
methodDescs.CML2002: European standard, widely used in academic and industrial LCA.
methodDescs.EF31: Required for EU-PEF and green procurement.
methodDescs.ILCD: EU JRC recommended method for European products.
methodDescs.GWP_only: Climate impact only (carbon footprint). Based on IPCC AR6 (2021).

step7.categoriesHelper: GWP is mandatory. Select additional categories relevant to your product.
step7.normalizationHelper: Optional ISO element. Relates results to a reference system (e.g. European average).
step7.weightingHelper: Optional ISO element — only where explicitly justified. ⚠️ ISO 14044 warns against over-interpretation of weighted results.

step8.title: Step 8: Interpretation
step8.subtitle: Identify hotspots, run sensitivity analysis and document conclusions.
step8.sensitivityParams_list: lifetime / recycling rate / use-phase energy / material quantities
step8.sensitivityPlaceholder: e.g. 'Conclusion robust for lifetime ±50%; electricity mix has the greatest influence'
step8.conclusionsHelper: What does the LCA show concretely? Use quantitative figures from the results.
step8.conclusionsPlaceholder: e.g. 'Product A has 40x lower GWP than alternative B, primarily driven by material choices in the raw material phase...'
step8.recommendationsPlaceholder: e.g. 'Prioritise recycled materials in the raw material phase. Increase EOL take-back.'
step8.limitationsHelper: What does the LCA NOT cover? What are the central uncertainties?
step8.limitationsPlaceholder: e.g. 'The user's smartphone is not included. Real-world adoption rate assumed to be 100%.'

step9.title: Step 9: Critical Review
step9.notRequired: Not required at current ambition level
step9.required: Critical review required at ambition level
step9.updateNote: Update ambition level in step 1 to activate.

step10.title: Step 10: Compliance Check
step10.subtitle: Automatic validation against ISO 14044's 15 mandatory elements.

exportReport.title: Export report
exportReport.includeAppendices: Include appendices (LCI tables, DQI matrix, sensitivity analysis)
exportReport.showDqi: Show DQI pedigree matrix in report

exportReport.generateError: Could not generate the PDF. Restart the dev server (npm run dev) and run: npx prisma migrate deploy
